issues.description: setPolicyType silently returns without setting a default when invalid policy is passed. It logs a warning but doesn't fall back to a valid policy type, potentially leaving the UI in an inconsistent state.
addManualItem validates item.id exists but doesn't check for duplicate IDs or validate the ID format. Could lead to multiple items with same ID in manualItems array.
activeTab is not persisted to localStorage. While not explicitly required by the feature description, persisting the last active tab would improve UX.
The detectedItems Map is initialized with a new Map() but this creates a new Map instance on every module reload. Consider documenting this is intentional for HMR compatibility.

sharedStateObservations.description: The worker discovered a Tailwind CSS v4 import issue during implementation - the @import 'tailwindcss' syntax works, but the worker had to debug CSS loading. This knowledge about Tailwind v4 configuration could be documented in library/.

localStorage mocking pattern for Vitest + jsdom: The worker implemented a proper mock using Object.defineProperty with getItem/setItem/clear/removeItem vi.fn() mocks. This pattern should be documented as a reusable testing convention.

summary: The app-state-context feature is fully implemented and meets all requirements. The AppContext provides all required state (policyType, onboardingComplete, activeTab, detectedItems Map, manualItems array, selectedItemId) and actions (setPolicyType, completeOnboarding, setActiveTab, updateDetectedItems, addManualItem, removeManualItem, updateManualItem, setSelectedItem). localStorage persistence works correctly for policyType, onboardingComplete, and manualItems. The useAppContext hook properly throws when used outside a provider. 25 comprehensive tests cover all functionality including edge cases like corrupted localStorage and unavailable storage. All tests pass (143 total). Minor non-blocking issues: setPolicyType could fall back to default on invalid input, and addManualItem could validate for duplicate IDs.
